behavior_summary: U8P_VIVDAGR::look is a simple 'U8P_VIVDAGR::look' (examine) handler: when the player examines the object/NPC it displays a single player-facing text/bark "ceremonial dagger " and then suspends/ends the process.
There is no branching, no menu interaction, no global flag reads or writes, and no external function calls recorded.
The function's behavior is linear and purely descriptive — it appears to provide the short in-game description for an object labeled as a ceremonial dagger.
notable_control_flow: Linear sequence: enter U8P_VIVDAGR::look process -> emit single Bark "ceremonial dagger " -> suspend/terminate process.
No conditional branches, loops, or menu-driven paths are present.
open_questions: The displayed text contains a trailing space ("ceremonial dagger "); is this intentional (padding/concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?
Unknown nodes present in the raw indicate low-confidence remnants ("{" and "suspend"); do they represent any subtle behavior not captured by the structured Bark + suspend interpretation?

main_function.name: U8P_VIVDAGR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines/looks at the object/NPC; present a single descriptive text and then suspend the U8P_VIVDAGR::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses the 'U8P_VIVDAGR::look' (examine) action on the associated object/NPC.
Also callable by engine U8P_VIVDAGR::look-type triggers/fast-area entry that map to processType 0x0208.

function_map.name: U8P_VIVDAGR::look
function_map.role: Handles the object's U8P_VIVDAGR::look/examine event: emits description text and suspends.
